Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer
Sydney - Hybrid (2–3 days onsite)
$150k - $175k + Super 

An established organisation is looking for a Senior Data Engineer to help design and deliver modern, cloud-based data platforms that support analytics, reporting and advanced data use cases.

This is a senior, hands-on role working across data architecture, pipeline engineering and platform development within a Google Cloud environment. You’ll collaborate closely with architects, engineers and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, secure and high-performing data solutions.

What you’ll be doing:

Design and build scalable data pipelines on Google Cloud, ingesting data from APIs, operational systems and third-party platforms
Develop and optimise data processing workflows using BigQuery, Dataflow and Cloud Composer to support analytics and reporting
Design and implement data models and transformation frameworks to enable trusted, high-quality datasets for business users
Apply DevOps and engineering best practices to improve reliability, automation and performance across the data platform
Skills & experience:

5+ years’ experience in Data Engineering and modern cloud data platforms
Strong hands-on experience with GCP technologies such as BigQuery, Dataflow, Cloud Storage and Cloud Composer
Advanced SQL and experience with Python or similar programming languages for data processing
Experience designing scalable data architectures and enterprise data solutions
Experience building ETL/ELT pipelines and integrating data across multiple source systems
Strong understanding of data governance, security and data quality principles
If you're a Senior Data Engineer looking for a high-impact role where you can shape a modern GCP data platform end-to-end, apply now or contact Liam Griffiths directly at liam.griffiths@halcyonknights.com.au

Come join oneZero Financial Systems, a global leader in trade routing and execution technology. We empower banks, brokerages, and hedge funds with a highly customizable, low-latency trading platform trusted by 200+ financial institutions worldwide.




At oneZero, we combine cutting-edge technology with a collaborative, high-performance culture. We’re proud to be recognized as a place where people genuinely enjoy doing their best work:

Job Description
As Senior Product Manager – Institutional, you will work directly with customers, sales, consulting, and operations to identify and validate common needs of the banks, larger institutional brokers, and non-bank market makers that make up our institutional target market.  You’ll translate those needs into actionable production direction in harmony with our overall strategy and collaborate closely with development colleagues to build new and enhanced capabilities to meet customer needs in differentiable ways.  Based in Singapore, Sydney, or Canberra, you'll follow your product enhancements into production to ensure they’re having the desired positive impact and gather new input for further advancement of our solutions.


Key Roles and Responsibilities

Product Strategy & Discovery

Partner with Sales, Relationship Management, and key clients to deeply understand institutional trading workflows and pain points

Work with the Managing Director – Institutional to size market opportunities, organize feature requests into cohesive initiatives, and prioritize within the institutional roadmap

Conduct customer research and discovery—including on-site visits with institutional clients in the APAC region

Keep tabs on competitive advancements, disseminate that knowledge throughout the team, and consider it in your roadmap refinement.

Define & Deliver

Define business requirements for institutional trading capabilities including:

Active Trader Aggregation UI for FX Spot and Swaps

Algorithmic Order Management

Advanced Systematic Hedging

Pre- and Post-trade Analytics and Execution Benchmarks

Collaborate with technical design and development teams to create specifications and architectural approaches

Identify proven 3rd party resources, frameworks, and tools to accelerate delivery when appropriate

Leverage AI to rapidly prototype features, generate early client feedback, and support technical design

Execute & Launch

Partner with the development team to plan releases, triage issues, and develop test plans

Work with operations to define deployment and support requirements

Design and manage pilot programs to drive customer adoption

Create sales enablement materials and conduct training sessions with Relationship Managers and Marketing

Support Sales in pitching new functionality to prospective customers as one means to training them to sell new capabilities.

Own the Outcomes

Be accountable for adoption and usage targets for initiatives in your product portfolio

Measure success through client adoption and recurring revenue generation




Minimum Requirements

7+ years of product management or development management experience in institutional FX trading technology

Direct experience in one or more of the following areas:

FX pricing and risk management

ECN or Active Trading Aggregator GUI/workflow design

EOMS or algorithmic execution

FX data and analytics

Deep understanding of institutional FX market structure and client segments (banks, hedge funds, institutional brokers)

Prior experience working on a bank trading desk OR as a product manager serving institutional trading clients

Experience working in distributed, global product teams




Nice to Have

Familiarity with oneZero's technology stack or similar trading platforms

Experience of AI tools for rapid prototyping of new functionality, and assisting with project management tasks

Job Title: Head of Financial Services Growth and Strategy
Ad Text:
Step into an exhilarating journey with Velocity Frequent Flyer Virgin Australia’s award-winning loyalty program with over 13 million members and counting. We make rewards fast and meaningful from dream holidays and upgrades to retail, fuel, and everyday perks.

We’re looking for a Head of Financial Services Growth and Strategy to lead the next phase of growth across our financial services portfolio. This pivotal role will shape the long-term strategy, unlock new revenue opportunities and drive performance across existing and emerging financial services partnerships. Reporting to the GM Commercial, you’ll play a key role in accelerating growth, enhancing customer lifetime value and strengthening Velocity’s position as one of Australia’s most powerful loyalty ecosystems.

What you’ll be doing

As the strategic leader of Velocity’s financial services portfolio, you’ll define and deliver the roadmap that drives sustainable growth across our partnerships and propositions. You’ll identify new opportunities, optimise existing products and work cross-functionally to bring innovative financial services experiences to millions of Velocity members.

Key Responsibilities

Define and execute the financial services growth strategy, identifying new revenue streams, product adjacencies and partnership opportunities that expand the Velocity ecosystem.
Own the commercial performance of the financial services portfolio, delivering revenue, margin and customer value targets.
Identify, evaluate and launch new financial services propositions, including fintech partnerships and innovative commercial models.
Optimise the performance of existing financial services partnerships, unlocking additional value through pricing, portfolio mix and commercial strategy.
Drive data-led growth across acquisition, engagement and retention, embedding experimentation and continuous optimisation frameworks.
Lead the development of investment cases and commercial strategies supported by robust financial modelling and strategic insight.
Represent Velocity in senior partner engagements and complex commercial negotiations, building long-term, high-value relationships.
Lead and inspire a high-performing team while influencing senior stakeholders across Velocity and Virgin Australia to deliver strategic growth outcomes.

You’ll be great in this role if you:

Bring deep financial services expertise, ideally across credit, payments or fintech, with a track record of delivering commercial growth through strategic partnerships and innovative propositions.
Demonstrate strong commercial leadership and advanced sales capability, with experience negotiating and scaling high-value partnerships.
Have a sophisticated understanding of loyalty ecosystems, customer acquisition and engagement strategies, and how financial services products drive member value.
Combine strong business modelling and behavioural analytics capability to evaluate opportunities, shape strategy and optimise portfolio performance.
Thrive in agile, high-growth environments, influencing senior stakeholders and leading teams to deliver complex, enterprise-level initiatives.

What you’ll get from us:

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to):

Heavily discounted air travel for you and your loved ones (including $1000 worth of travel credits per year)
Flexible working arrangements (including work hours and work from home)
Discounts on travel insurance, car hire, accommodation and experiences worldwide
Discounted Virgin Australia Lounge membership
Hospitality, retail, technology, beauty services and wellness discounts
Wellness support, including the betterme digital wellbeing platform
A comprehensive Employee Assistance Program, which offers confidential coaching and support from qualified professionals for all aspects of life – physical, mental, social and financial
Dress for Your Day – enjoy the freedom to wear whatever is appropriate for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you

Equality rules:

We’re all individuals. We love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTQI+ community, and anyone else who wants to join our family.

Ready to apply? We’re ready to hear from you. Apply now.

As the Business Analyst, working in this daily rate contract role, you’ll drive a fast-paced rewards transformation and reshape how venues and customers engage with a new loyalty experience.

Why this role matters

Step into a role where your analysis powers a new Retail Rewards experience across venues and digital. Joining the Retail Programs team at Tabcorp, you will be the Business Analyst on a greenfield loyalty initiative, implementing a Software as a Service solution with a technology partner. Your work will shape the points model, redemption experience and the support model that will scale nationwide.

Your role, your impact

Lead discovery to document end to end retail and finance processes
Drive requirements for loyalty points allocation, redemption rules, and reporting
Create user stories, acceptance criteria, and process maps stakeholders trust
Support user acceptance testing, develop test scenarios, and triage defects
Coordinate venue pilots and feedback to refine experience and support model
Collaborate with retail and finance leads to design practical operating procedures

What you'll need to succeed

Experienced in Software as a Service (SaaS) implementations within complex retail environments
Proven ability to map processes and write clear, testable requirements
Strong capability with user acceptance testing, scenario design, and defect triage
Ability to work with business and finance leads on loyalty design
Skilled at turning data from users and venues into actionable insights
Comfortable operating in greenfield settings working with vendors

Who we are

TAB is part of Tabcorp, Australia’s leading gambling entertainment company. We’re an iconic brand trusted by millions, creating excitement across 4,400 venues nationwide as well as through our digital, phone and on-track channels. With over 1.1 billion bets taken each year, TAB is where Australians come for unforgettable moments - and where you can belong where the action is.

Join the action

Ready to make TAB your home ground? Join a team that thrives on collaboration, inclusion, respect, and face to face connection. We welcome and support people of all abilities - if you need adjustments during the process, email careers@tabcorp.com.au
